title: Crispy Korean Seafood Pancakes
description: A savory, crumbly pancake filled with fresh leeks and mixed seafood, finished with a tangy dipping sauce for a quick, satisfying bite.

ingredients.name: mixed seafood (shrimp, squid, mussels)
ingredients.notes: cleaned and cut into bite‑sized pieces

instructions: Whisk flour, gochugaru, and salt in a large bowl. Gradually whisk in cold water, soy sauce, and sesame oil until the batter is smooth with a few small lumps. Let rest 5–10 minutes.
While the batter rests, prepare the seafood: cut shrimp into small pieces, slice squid rings, and clean mussels.
Slice leeks into thin half‑moons and chop green onions, garlic, ginger, and cilantro.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat and add 1 tbsp of sesame oil, swirling to coat the surface.
Pour a ladleful of batter into the skillet, spreading it thin. Sprinkle evenly with seafood, leeks, green onions, garlic, ginger, and cilantro.

Cook until the edges become crisp and the bottom turns golden brown, about 2–3 minutes. Flip carefully with a spatula and cook the other side until golden and the seafood is cooked through, about 2 minutes.
Remove the pancake from the skillet and repeat with the remaining batter and ingredients.
Slice the pancakes into wedges and serve hot with a dipping sauce made from 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp gochujang (optional), 1 tsp sesame oil, sliced chili, and chopped scallions.
